title: dicliptera rauhii
intro: Dicliptera rauhii, a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, is commonly known as the "Sage of the Andes" due to its native habitat in the Andean regions of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dicliptera rauhii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in herbal remedies such as tinctures and ointments.
